title: CellROX Green Live Imaging Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ddg3i","text":"This protocol is designed to assess reactive oxygen species (ROS) levels in live cells using the CellROX Green reagent, enabling real-time imaging of oxidative stress in cellular environments.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"67h9o","text":"Handle all reagents with care, using appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE). Dispose of all waste according to institutional safety guidelines.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text:
{"blocks":[{"key":"68bhr","text":"Complete Culture Medium (DMEM without phenol red and riboflavin, supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% pen/strep) - 10 mL per well
CellROX Green Reagent (2.5 mM in DMSO) - 10 µL (for 1:1000 dilution in 10 mL medium)
Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS) (pH 7.4) - 10 mL (for washing)
96-well plastic-bottom plates (µ clear; Greiner Bio-One) - 1 plate

Reagents:
Fluorescent Dye CellROX Green - A cell-permeable probe that fluoresces upon oxidation by ROS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Incubate the cells at 37 °C for 30 minutes, protected from light.
Remove the staining medium. Replace with fresh, pre-warmed medium.
Image the cells immediately using an epifluorescence or confocal microscope with a FITC/GFP-like channel (excitation ~485 nm, emission ~520 nm).
Capture images of the cells to assess ROS levels.

Warm complete culture medium to 37 °C. Dilute CellROX Green reagent directly into the culture medium , resulting in a final concentration of 1µM. Note: for microglia we change half medium only, so the conentration of the solution will be 2µM, to have 1µM as final concentration on cells
creator: Elena Coccia
